title: Playtest case file
description: One test case (<name>.yaml): a journey (regression with a deterministic gate) or a discovery study (open-ended exploration, no gate). Any key left unset inherits from the nearest playtest.yaml up the directory chain (nearest wins). max_steps/timeout may also be spelled nested under limits.

story.description: Required. The user's goal in natural language, addressed to the actor in the second person ("You keep forgetting to buy milk..."). Journey stories name concrete targets in double quotes; discovery stories state a goal without prescribing the path ("Get your timesheet data out of the platform however seems natural").

tags.description: Free-form labels for --tag filtering, e.g. [smoke]. Case-only: not allowed in playtest.yaml. Default [].

persona.description: Persona the actor role-plays: built-in "tester" or "exploratory", or the name/slug of a personas/*.yaml file. Default "tester". Inheritable. Discovery cases usually use the plural "personas" instead.

personas.description: Discovery-only fan-out: the case expands into one run per persona listed (same name/slug resolution as "persona"), each with id <case-id>@<persona> and the singular "persona" overridden. Config error in a journey-mode case or in playtest.yaml.

mode.description: Case kind, default "journey". journey: regression test — deterministic success gate, baseline record/act/heal, pass/fail exit codes. discovery: product-insight study — no success gate, always a fresh agentic run that ends "explored" (give_up is a valid, informative outcome), graded with the discoverability rubric. Inheritable: a study directory usually sets mode: discovery once in its playtest.yaml.

url_matches.description: Glob (* and ?) matched against the final URL or its pathname, e.g. "/done/*".

element_exists.description: CSS selector that must match on the final page, e.g. "[data-testid=todo-item]".

api_called.description: "METHOD /path/glob" that must appear in the run's network traffic, e.g. "POST /api/todos" or "PATCH /api/todos/*".

assert.description: Natural-language claim about the final page, checked by the grader model. Costs one model call per run, even on acted runs.

success.description: Journey-only deterministic gate: every criterion must pass, each entry exactly one of url_matches/element_exists/api_called/assert. Case-only: not allowed in playtest.yaml. Config error in a discovery case (ask report: questions instead). Default [].

lcp_ms.description: Threshold on the WORST navigation LCP in milliseconds: "< 2500" style (ops < <= > >=) or a bare number meaning <= n.

input_to_paint_ms.description: Threshold on the WORST input-to-paint latency in milliseconds; same format as lcp_ms.

console_errors.description: Maximum allowed console error count, e.g. 0.

perf.description: Perf thresholds checked with the journey gate. Inheritable. Silently irrelevant in discovery (the gate never runs).

report.description: Discovery report questions the grader must answer from the trajectory, e.g. "At which screen would this user have expected an export affordance?". Questions, not assertions — findings have answers, not pass/fail. Case-only: not allowed in playtest.yaml. Default [].

max_steps.description: Maximum actor steps before the run ends with end_reason max_steps. Default 50. Inheritable. Equivalent nested spelling: limits.max_steps.

timeout.description: Wall-clock limit per run: "5m", "90s", "250ms", or a number of milliseconds. Default "4m". Inheritable. Equivalent nested spelling: limits.timeout.

limits.description: Nested spelling of max_steps/timeout; normalized to the top-level keys at load time.

actor_model.description: Model that role-plays the user. Default "claude-haiku-4-5" (pinned cheap — for journeys the app is the variable, not the agent). Discovery studies often choose a stronger model, e.g. "claude-sonnet-4-6". Inheritable.

grader_model.description: Model that grades finished runs and checks assert: criteria. Default "claude-sonnet-4-6". Inheritable.

base_url.description: Where the app under test runs, e.g. http://localhost:4173. Required somewhere in the chain (or pass --base-url). Agents really click buy/delete/submit — point at staging or test, never production.

compose.description: docker-compose file path, relative to this file: managed mode — the harness boots and tears down the app per run.

init.description: Executable script path, relative to this file, run before each case to reset app state.

storage_state.description: Playwright storage-state JSON path, relative to this file, for a pre-authenticated browser session.

app.description: App under test. Merges per-key down the defaults chain, so a case can override just base_url.
